method_atom: A symmetry-based derivation showing that a fluctuating system with a single dynamical degree of freedom obeys a universal identity (a 'consistency relation') constraining its three-point correlation function in a soft/squeezed limit — where one mode's wavenumber is taken to zero — so that it is fixed by the two-point function. The relation is proven both from the underlying symmetry structure and, independently and explicitly, via an effective field theory expansion of the fluctuations to leading nontrivial order in a set of small expansion parameters.
object_atom: The primordial curvature/temperature perturbations imprinted on the cosmic microwave background, generated by a single dynamical scalar (inflaton) field driving single-field inflation in the early universe.
brief_reason: The squeezed-limit identity forcing the three-point function of a single-degree-of-freedom field to be determined by its two-point function, derivable via a symmetry argument and an effective-field-theory expansion, is the transferable method; the CMB perturbations from single-field inflation are the cosmology-specific object.
